title: ranunculus komarowii
intro: Ranunculus komarowii, also known as the Komarov's buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This perennial plant is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains alkaloids and glycosides with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Ranunculus komarowii are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or decoctions for internal use, and the plant's extracts are also used topically to promote wound healing and reduce skin inflammation.
